Surrounded by early summer sun, crystal clear water, and quiet Mediterranean landscapes, Felice stepped in front of Khalil’s camera for the first time, turning their relationship into a story of its own.

posts.caption: Last week, creative directors, gallerists, and actors were among those to spend two days in New York’s Adirondack Mountains at Camp Crew, the adult summer camp that is the backdrop for @JCrew’s latest brand trip and campaign. @JCrew CMO @Julia__Collier sat down with US editor @MaddieeSchulz to unpack the marketing of Americana in modern times. Tap the link in bio to read more.
posts.alt_text:
Social media has hit a roadblock. This week, the UK government announced a social media ban for under-16s, effective next year. It’s the latest sign that the anti-social media movement is reaching a tipping point, as the push to limit children’s exposure to addictive algorithms and other potential online harms extends beyond activist interests into the mainstream.

Brands and marketers will need to adapt to how they reach these consumers, likely resulting in a much more fragmented marketing strategy. Experts say multiplayer games like Roblox and Fortnite — where under-16s are socializing more — could become an appealing alternative for brands. Tap the link in bio for a detailed history of social media.

Photo: @ghostindress

posts.caption: Last week, Hugo Boss’s largest shareholder, Frasers Group, announced plans to launch a takeover bid. The timing is notable. @Boss is no longer the brand Daniel Grieder inherited in 2021: sales have doubled and the company has moved into the next phase of its Claim 5 strategy, focused on building brand equity and improving profitability. If the deal goes ahead, the question is not whether Hugo Boss needs a new strategy — but whether full @FrasersGroup ownership would help complete the one already underway. @KirstyMcGregorEdits has all the details at the link in bio.

Smriti Mandhana (@smriti_mandhana) invites Vogue to her hometown in Sangli, Maharashtra, where life moves at its own pace. In between tours, the record-breaking vice-captain of the Indian women’s cricket team returns to sugar cane fields, school grounds and familiar routines.

World Cup winner, WPL champion, ICC number one ODI batter and now a Barbie in her likeness, May cover star Smriti Mandhana’s (@smriti_mandhana) world has expanded far beyond Sangli. Back home, though, her friends don’t really bite into the whole celebrity thing. “Lagta hai aaj tune kuch kiya hai?” they tease when a reel of her latest milestone shows up on their Instagram feed.
Read the full cover story at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.
She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”
Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.
For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile.
Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.
posts.alt_text:

Every item in her home has been intentionally selected, curated, and has a story. Head to the link in our bio to watch the full video, and to take the full tour of Burch's Parisian paradise.

Gaffer @skinnylapuissance
Audio: Hubert Rey-Grange

posts.caption:
Maria Galligani caught the Norwegian tennis player @CasperRuud’s eye when they grew up near each other in the suburbs of Oslo. It wasn’t until March 2018 that the two were officially introduced by Casper’s best friend, fellow tennis player Joachim Bjerke, at an Oslo nightclub. By September, they would make it official on a trip together to Mallorca.
The couple took their next big step in their relationship while visiting the Maldives in November 2024. Casper proposed after the pair spent the day enjoying the beach. “When we returned to the little beach bungalow where we were staying, we played a game of backgammon, which Casper won—as usual,” says Maria, who works as a psychologist and helps with her family’s tabletop business. The couple decided to tie the knot in Mallorca, where they went on their first trip together and both wore Norwegian designers for their celebrations. From the welcome party to a sports day with a golf tournament, yoga, and garden party, see more from their nuptials at the link in bio. Photos: @ElineJacobine

posts.caption: France's #WorldCup team is in their bag—whether it be @chanelofficial, @louisvuitton, or @hermes. During their trip to the U.S. for the global tournament, the French National Team—known as “Les Bleus”—showed off a bag collection that had us saying, well, “Oh la la.” Ahead of their match against Senegal today, tap the link in our bio for every detail on the team's insanely luxurious and perfectly patriotic assortment of bags.

Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.

She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”

Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.

For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile. 

Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption: Call it a full-circle moment for @NYKnicks captain Jalen Brunson, a noted Justin Bieber fan. This week, Hailey Bieber joined in on the celebration of the team's NBA Finals win without breaking out the royal blue and orange. Instead, she sported a vintage NYC sweatshirt paired with classic blue jeans. Get all the details on her and Justin's looks at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
Michael Rider’s work at @Celine has been celebrated for merging the wearable sophistication of Pheobe Philo’s era with the sportswear-inflected panache of his own time at Ralph Lauren, he’s actually created a new chapter entirely his own—one that feels joyous and (even in our current moment) wildly optimistic. With three acclaimed collections, Rider is doing what Philo and Hedi Slimane did before him: Celine is once more fashion’s most-wanted label. Tap the link in our bio to read more about Rider and the new era of Celine.
Photographed by @francescplanes, Written by @christbollen, Styled by @malinajoseph, Hair by @karimbelghiran, Makeup by @karinwesterlundd, Manicures by @nailsbymagda.s, Tailoring by @instopalami, Produced by @nilmproduction. Vogue, Summer 2026.

Tap the link in bio to watch.

posts.caption:
Eight years after his first tenure at @Celine, where he served as design director under Phoebe Philo, Michael Rider returned to the house—and in the time since, has delivered three acclaimed collections. His coveted spring line is in stores, and his fall collection received a rapturous reception when it was shown in March. Rider has certainly put a stop to any lingering questions as to how a curious kid from Washington, DC, ended up running one of the most quintessentially Parisian of fashion houses. Head to the link in bio for more on how Rider is remaking the French label with a unique mix of joy, optimism, and hit accessories.
Photographed by @annieleibovitz, Written by @christbollen, Styled by @jackborkett, Grooming by @jillianhalouska, Set Design by @maryhoward_setdesign, Produced by AL Studio. Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption:
As former model Chris Royer explained to Vogue, the best way to understand what it was like to work with Duane Michals is to use the Hungarian term kincsvadászat, which essentially means a “treasure hunt.” For Royer, a Duane Michals story was exactly that: “You were going on a trip, you knew you were going to discover things, it was a creative treasure hunt.” Michals who passed away on June 9th,  contributed regularly to Vogue during the 1970s and ’80s. His work during that period ranged from shooting stills of Robert Redford and Mia Farrow in costume on set while filming The Great Gatsby; to reportage of the San Francisco ballet and jewelry designer Elsa Peretti hard at work in her legendary studio-apartment; to portraits of notable men, including Yves Saint Laurent, Dudley Moore, and Philip Glass. Take a look back at his legacy at the link in bio.
1) Photographed by Duane Michals, Vogue, October 1976
2) Photographed by Duane Michals, Vogue, February 1976
3) Photographed by Duane Michals, Vogue, April 15, 1972
4) Photographed by Duane Michals, Vogue, October 1976
5) Photographed by Duane Michals, Vogue, October 1976

posts.caption: While watching the 2026 NBA Finals at home, you may have noticed a dapper older gentleman in an outrageous suit among the stars. That would be @NYKnicks Legend Walt “Clyde” Frazier, who led the team to two championships in the 1970s. His singular approach to dressing has been carefully chronicled and graded (on a letter scale) by the site “ClydesoFly.com,” going back to 2013. Naturally, he doesn’t buy off the rack. Frazier’s suits were made by tailors in New York, mostly from fabric he plucked from shops in then Garment District and Lower East Side. Learn more about his dapper ensembles at the link in bio.

posts.caption: Major change is afoot in Sweden, a fact highlighted by the two shows that bookended Stockholm Fashion Week, by provocative and kinky Gullbo on one side and the ruminatively minimalist Leoní on the other. Not only did these collections represent two vastly different ends of an aesthetic spectrum, but they also marked a generational shift (which was also evident in street style). At the link in bio, find a lowdown of the interesting, youthful, melange that was STHLMFW 2026.

posts.caption: Everyone has their favorite pair of sneakers—whether that be a sporty trainer, a comfortable canvas, or one with a retro feel—but no collection is complete without one (or two!) in white. Whether you’re searching for fashion or function, head to the link in our bio to shop Vogue's edit of the best white sneakers available right now. Photographed by @jancrlo
posts.alt_text: As we start to look for our warm-weather pieces, we’re asking ourselves, “How would the Olsen twins prepare for the season?” They would let proportion do the talking, with oversized cotton poplin blouses, languid trousers, and flouncy skirts. In addition to laid-back flops, they might also pull out an unexpected pair of crochet ballet flats or beaded sandals that embody the essence of the season. The key? Start with the basics and build from there. Tap the link in our bio to shop and recreate their chicest looks.

posts.caption: One of the silver linings of being a frequent traveler—beyond the miles accrued—is that packing isn’t so much a chore as it is muscle memory. “I have it down to a science,” says Ilona Hamer, co-founder of Australian resortwear label Matteau of her dialed system. Last year alone, the stylist and consultant spent about 250 days on the go. At the link in bio, for the latest installment of #MyPackingStyle, Hamer shares what she packs for a holiday in the sun.

posts.caption: If summer’s sandals trends share a common thread, it’s a refusal to choose between any one aesthetic. Designers have traded the exclusively minimalist mood of recent summers for options that encourage self-expression. Whether it’s Le Monde Béryl’s pale blue beaded sandals, Gucci’s sporty heeled thongs, or Ancient Greek Sandals’ jelly flops, the chicest sandal trends of 2026 are personal rather than prescriptive. Discover them all at the link in bio.

posts.caption: “The best evenings always have a little mix of the familiar and the unexpected,” says @arussotti, founder and creative director of Brooklyn-based decor brand @Porta_nyc. “I’ve never once remembered a dinner because everything was perfect. I remember it because it felt lovely and nobody wanted to leave.” Recently, on one of the first truly warm June evenings, she put this entertaining philosophy into practice with an intimate backyard party in Brooklyn’s Boerum Hill. She enlisted the help of Arroces Street Paella to whip up two varieties of the dish, so she could spend the evening mingling with guests. Discover all of her tips and tricks for hosting this summer at the link in bio.

posts.alt_text:
A stage. A story. A Rajkumari. 

RAJKUMARI is back, MY BIGGEST INDIA TOUR YET. (If you don’t see your city don’t worry, more cities to be added). 
Tickets on Book My Show.

What is Rajkumari?

An evening of stories, poems dipped in music. This is not just a show. It’s a gentle rebellion. Against everything you were told to be.A space to see differently.
To live differently. Your way. A reminder of the power of your own perspective, of your “Nayab Nazariya”.

After touring in 6 countries, Rajkumari is back in India. Come for one of India’s biggest storytelling & Poetry show.

Tickets on @bookmyshowin , link in my bio🔗

✨ Creator, Actor, PHENOMENON! ✨ On the June-July cover of Wedding Vows, we present the trailblazing @mostlysane, Prajakta Koli!
From building a YouTube empire to being the lead for Netflix Original Mismatched, from being part of Michelle Obama’s Day Time Emmy winning docu-series to being UNDP India's First Youth Climate Champion, her journey is nothing short of extraordinary.
With her debut romance novel set to launch this year, along with her first Amazon Prime Original Andhera, Prajakta’s journey is a testament to the power of dreams, hard work, and staying true to oneself. She continues to inspire millions with her authenticity, relatability, and unstoppable spirit.

posts.caption:
@camimendes is having one of the biggest years in her career so far. A major summer blockbuster, the second feature film from her production company, and wedding planning are all on the roster for 2026. This month, Mendes steps into the sprawling world of Masters of the Universe as the sword-slinging Teela alongside Nicholas Galitzine, Idris Elba, and Jared Leto.
The project is a stretch from her days as Veronica Lodge in #Riverdale, which catapulted her into mainstream recognition nearly a decade ago. Mendes makes movies for “the girls and the gays,” she states lovingly, but her star power is only going to get bigger and brighter with Masters. “I’ve never been a part of something of this scale,” she says of the project.
In our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, we chat with the actress about reinventing herself in this era of her life. Read it all in the full interview at the link in our bio.
photographer: @gleesonpaulino
stylist: @laurenegg
creative direction: @arrrrmani
hair stylist: @laurenpalmersmith
makeup artist: @jentioseco
manicurist: @thuybnguyen
entertainment director: @jbake21
writer: @balencianas
producer: @_porcelainchaos @delafe_pr
special thanks to @blackwidowmustang & @goldlinebar
posts.alt_text:
There would be no Pope Heyward without @jonathandavissofficial. Much like his smart, careful, deeply moral Outer Banks character, Daviss knows what it feels like to carry the pressure of proving your worth and refusing to let yourself or anyone else down. “What really stood out to me about Pope in season 1 was this idea of expectations and pressure that gets put on a lot of young Black teens and teens in general,” he says. “There’s this pressure to succeed, and it was something I truly have been through.”

Daviss still thinks back to his early audition days before landing the life-changing role on OBX, when the constant need to prove himself as a young actor felt emotionally exhausting. His mindset then? “You only lose when you quit.”

Head to the link in our bio for the full download in our Now Playing Issue presented by @bvlgari, from what to expect from the fifth and final season of OBX to his upcoming portrayal of a young Snoop Dogg.

posts.caption:
@victoriamonet is trading her claws for butterfly wings. The three-time Grammy winner is the definition of a multi-hyphenate: a culinary school graduate, a mother, and, of course, a singer-songwriter with a nearly two-decade-spanning catalog to her name. Now, using her 37th birthday as a launching pad, Monét has cleared her mental space and scrubbed her socials to prepare for her next chapter—an album she’s dubbed her very own Thriller, a sonically surprising, genre-defying project that marks a softer evolution. “The jaguar exists within me always, but I’m feeling like a softer version of myself now,” she says.
After all, Monét’s critically acclaimed debut studio album Jaguar II was the ultimate learning lesson. “Once you have an accolade like that, people often look for a reason to discredit why you got it,” she explains. “I think—pending a Grammy happens again—I would do the opposite and bunker down in the studio.”
Step inside Monét’s world in our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
Monaco! Miami! Mundt! The stylist behind many of the @f1 WAG’s looks, @natrosenfeldstyling, is giving us an exclusive look into styling @carmenmmundt during race season. “Working alongside [her] for the past three years has been such a natural and rewarding experience,” Rosenfeld shares with us. “Our relationship has always felt incredibly organic.” From fittings to races, the two have formed a relationship that has “built a strong level of trust and understanding.” We’re excited to see what the two have in store for the Austrian Grand Prix. Shop the looks at the link in our bio. #GoSports
photos: getty images, @natrosenfeldstyling, @carmenmmundt
posts.alt_text:
@camimendes is having one of the biggest years in her career so far. A major summer blockbuster, the second feature film from her production company, and wedding planning are all on the roster for 2026. This month, Mendes steps into the sprawling world of Masters of the Universe as the sword-slinging Teela alongside Nicholas Galitzine, Idris Elba, and Jared Leto. 

The project is a stretch from her days as Veronica Lodge in #Riverdale, which catapulted her into mainstream recognition nearly a decade ago. Mendes makes movies for “the girls and the gays,” she states lovingly, but her star power is only going to get bigger and brighter with Masters. “I’ve never been a part of something of this scale,” she says of the project. 

In our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, we chat with the actress about reinventing herself in this era of her life. Read it all in the full interview at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
As a warmup ahead of his couture debut for @Balenciaga on July 8, creative director Pierpaolo Piccioli (@pppiccioli) is on a wellness kick.
In tandem with the launch of the Balenciaga Techwear line, an integral element in his sportif pre-fall 2026 collection, the Paris-based house is planning pop-ups in Paris, Beijing, Shanghai and Bangkok that will be serving up smoothies, healthy juices and post-workout protein and adaptogen bars.
In addition, the Paris-based fashion house has planned full-day takeovers at Barry’s fitness studios in Los Angeles, New York City, London, Paris, Milan, Dubai, Singapore and Sydney.
Billed as a first for Barry’s, founded in West Hollywood in 1998, the partnership with Balenciaga spans customized fitness classes with special guests, curated playlists, special decor and a co-branded smoothie at the Fuel Bar.
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @milessocha

From generative AI to geopolitical tensions, the seismic shifts that are roiling the world are also having a significant impact on beauty.
The result: Another challenging year for many of beauty’s biggest players, as evidenced by the results of the 2025 Beauty Inc Top 100, our annual ranking of the world’s largest beauty manufacturers by sales.
In the five years of turbulence since the COVID-19 pandemic, the market landscape has shifted significantly, and the ongoing headwinds saw many companies take a closer look at their operations as they seek to press reset.
And no wonder. During 2025, skin care struggled for many. Much of the mass market was in the doldrums, and growth in fragrance slowed for all but a handful of players. Total sales for all 100 companies in our ranking reached $258.99 billion, up 2.7% year-over-year. There were pockets of strong growth, but by and large, the market was lackluster at best.
Read more on the 2025 Top 100 beauty compagnies at WWD.com - #linkinbio
Report : @al_wynne @kathryn.hopkins @noorlobaddie @jamesmanso @samanthavconti @sandrasalibian @kaleighwerner @dennihu Mayu Saini & Faye Brookman

The latest guest designer at Pitti Uomo is neither a heritage house nor a promising newcomer throwing their hat in the sartorial ring.
If anything, DSM Kei Ninomiya is a UFO.
First, by virtue of its founding principle as a newfangled take on private label merchandise from the Dover Street Market retail emporium that is the brainchild of Rei Kawakubo and Adrian Joffe. At the brand’s launch, it came with a tagline that read “Untitled, Untethered, Undefined.”
Read more on Pitti Uomo at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @lilytempleton
Photography: @hugoveuillet

The limited-edition collection, “French Girl Summer by Maison Perrier,” features more than 50 curated looks and debuts today.
Learn More at #linkinbio

Returning to a gobsmacking natural setting for his next fashion show, Simon Porte @Jacquemus has selected a rocky island off Corsica whose main landmark is a small, white lighthouse.
The coed collection for spring-summer 2027, titled “Le Bonheur,” is to be unveiled at 10 a.m. on June 29.
“I am deeply honored and proud to present this show at the Phare de la Pietra in Île-Rousse, a place of exceptional beauty and history,” Jacquemus said in a statement shared exclusively with WWD.
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @milesocha

NEW RECRUIT: @BrunoSialelli, who rose to prominence as creative director of @Lanvin between 2019 and 2023, recently joined @balenciaga couture studio, according to market sources. His title and start date could not immediately be learned.
Contacted by WWD on Friday, Balenciaga said the house doesn’t comment on the composition of its studio. Sialelli did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
Pierpaolo Piccioli (@pppiccioli), appointed Balenciaga’s creative director last July, is set to show his first haute couture collection on July 8 & during Paris Couture Week. (Piccioli’s the most anticipated debut of the week, given his long experience and widespread acclaim at @Valentino in Rome.)
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @milessocha
